seoTitle: Conroe ISD Calendar 2025-2026: Holidays, Breaks & PDF
seoDescription: Conroe ISD 2025-2026 calendar with Aug 13 first day, May 22 last day, student holidays, Thanksgiving, winter break, spring break, PDF and ICS.

calendarFaqs.q: When does school start for Conroe ISD in 2025-2026?
calendarFaqs.a: The first day of school for Conroe Independent School District in 2025-2026 is Wednesday, August 13, 2025 — one of the earlier start dates among major Houston-area districts. Back-to-school preparation should be complete by the second week of August. Families arriving from states that begin school in September should verify the August 13 date before booking end-of-summer travel; plan to be back in the Greater Houston area by August 9–10 to allow time before the first day.

calendarFaqs.q: When is the last day of school for Conroe ISD in 2025-2026?
calendarFaqs.a: The last day of school for Conroe Independent School District in 2025-2026 is Friday, May 22, 2026 — an early release day covering 172 instructional days. Memorial Day (May 25) falls after the school year ends, so there is no separate Memorial Day no-school day during the school year. Summer programs and childcare starting May 25 or later align with the Conroe ISD end date without a gap.

calendarFaqs.q: When is Spring Break for Conroe ISD in 2025-2026, and how does it compare to Houston ISD?
calendarFaqs.a: Spring Break for Conroe Independent School District in 2025-2026 runs Monday, March 9 through Friday, March 13, 2026 — five days. Conroe ISD and Houston ISD set their calendars independently, and in 2025-2026 Conroe ISD's break falls in early March — typically earlier than Houston ISD's window. When the two breaks fall in different weeks, Conroe ISD families departing during the March 9 week may find better IAH availability and fares than during the Harris County peak. Verify both calendars before booking travel or childcare that depends on both districts.

calendarFaqs.q: Does Conroe ISD have Good Friday and Easter Monday off in 2025-2026?
calendarFaqs.a: Yes. In 2025-2026, Conroe ISD observes both Good Friday (April 3) and Easter Monday (April 6) as Student Holidays, creating a four-day Easter break with no school Friday through Monday, April 3–6. This arrangement is not standard across Texas ISDs and does not appear in the 2026-2027 Conroe ISD calendar. Families planning Easter weekend travel should note the Monday April 6 no-school day. Neighboring districts (Klein ISD, Spring ISD) may or may not observe either holiday — verify separately if coordinating across districts.

calendarFaqs.q: When does Winter Break end for Conroe ISD in 2025-2026, and when do students return?
calendarFaqs.a: Winter Break for Conroe ISD in 2025-2026 runs Monday, December 22, 2025 through Monday, January 5, 2026 — 15 days. Students return Tuesday, January 6, 2026. The semester also closes with an early release day on December 19 — the last instructional day of the first semester before Winter Break. George Bush Intercontinental Airport (IAH) sees peak holiday traffic the weekend before December 22 and the days after New Year's; book holiday flights early once the calendar is confirmed.

calendarFaqs.q: Why does Conroe ISD have 172 instructional days instead of 180?
calendarFaqs.a: Texas Education Agency guidelines allow districts to structure calendars around required annual instructional minutes (75,600 minutes per year) rather than a strict 180-day count. Conroe ISD operates under this minutes-based framework and meets all TEA requirements, resulting in 172 student days while satisfying state law. Early release days — when school dismisses several hours early — count toward required minutes at a proportional rate. The TEA's official district accountability report for Conroe ISD is available at tea.texas.gov for families who want to verify the district's compliance with state instructional requirements.

yearNumbers.label: Good Friday + Easter
yearNumbers.detail: Conroe ISD observes both Good Friday (April 3) and Easter Monday (April 6) as Student Holidays in 2025–2026, creating a four-consecutive-day break — Friday through Monday — with no school. This is an unusual arrangement among Texas ISDs; most districts observe either Good Friday alone or neither day. Klein ISD and Spring ISD do not both observe this combination in 2025–2026, so families coordinating with neighboring-district relatives should verify each calendar. Families planning Easter weekend travel should note that departure demand from IAH peaks Thursday evening, April 2 — book before mid-February to avoid the holiday flight surge from the Greater Houston metro.

yearNumbers.label: Spring Break
yearNumbers.detail: Conroe ISD's Spring Break in 2025–2026 runs March 9–13 — typically one week earlier than Houston ISD's spring break window. When the two districts break in different weeks, IAH departure demand is split rather than concentrated, which can translate to marginally better availability and pricing the week Conroe ISD is out. Families with children in both Conroe ISD and Houston ISD during the same year — common in blended households or after mid-year relocations — will face two separate break windows that require separate childcare arrangements. Book flights and hotels for the March 9 week by January; the March window is also Austin SXSW season, which affects AUS and DFW more than IAH but compresses statewide rental car supply.

yearNumbers.label: Twin No-School Pairs
yearNumbers.detail: Conroe ISD 2025–2026 includes two paired no-school stretches in the fall semester that are easy to miss if you're only tracking federal holidays. October 10 and 13 create a long Columbus Day weekend — no school Friday and Monday, in addition to the Columbus Day federal holiday — giving families a four-day break in early October. November 3–4 are consecutive Student Holidays added about three weeks before Thanksgiving, creating a second mini-break that is entirely district-specific and not visible on any standard federal holiday calendar. Both pairs fall outside the standard back-to-school planning window (September) and catch families off guard who haven't downloaded and marked the official CISD calendar for the year.

yearNumbers.label: Winter Break
yearNumbers.detail: Winter Break in Conroe ISD 2025–2026 runs 15 calendar days, from Monday December 22 through Monday January 5 — students return Tuesday, January 6. The first semester closes with an early release on December 19, so the effective break starts the Friday before Winter Break officially begins. IAH holiday flight demand peaks the weekend before December 22 and again the days immediately after New Year's; book round-trip holiday travel before the Thanksgiving week if possible, as Houston-area departure demand in late December is among the highest of the year. Summer programs and childcare that start January 6 align with the Conroe ISD return date without a gap.

events.name: Last Day of First Semester / Student Early Release
Last Day of School / Student Early Release

calendarNotes:
Conroe ISD begins the 2025–2026 school year on Wednesday, August 13, 2025 — one of the earlier starts among major Houston-area districts. Texas Education Agency guidelines require 75,600 annual instructional minutes rather than a strict 180-day count; Conroe ISD meets this requirement across 172 student days. The mid-August start overlaps with peak Texas summer heat: August temperatures in Montgomery County regularly exceed 100°F, and UIL heat protocols restrict outdoor athletic practices during extreme heat. Families arriving from northern or western states where school begins in September often underestimate how early back-to-school preparation must begin.

The fall semester runs without a dedicated fall break week. Student Holidays — no-school days used for teacher professional development, grading, or campus preparation — are distributed across the semester instead: October 10 and 13 create a long Columbus Day weekend, and November 3–4 add two consecutive no-school days about three weeks before Thanksgiving. Thanksgiving Break covers the full school week (November 24–28), and students return December 1. These scattered Student Holidays accumulate quickly and fall outside the standard federal holiday calendar; download the official calendar and mark each one separately before September.

The 2025–2026 calendar includes an Easter break that is unusually structured among Texas ISDs: both Good Friday (April 3) and Easter Monday (April 6) are Student Holidays, creating four consecutive no-school days. Most Texas districts observe Good Friday alone or neither day; Conroe ISD's four-day Easter break is not replicated in the 2026–2027 calendar, making it a year-specific planning consideration. Spring Break runs March 9–13, and the combination of Spring Break and the April Easter break means the spring semester has two extended no-school stretches within about four weeks.

First semester closes December 19 (early release day); second semester begins January 6, 2026 after a 15-day Winter Break. The school year ends May 22, 2026 — also an early release day. Memorial Day (May 25) falls after school ends. Conroe ISD high school graduation ceremonies are typically held in late May, with individual school dates announced by spring; the week following the last day of school is among the busiest at Lake Conroe and George Bush Intercontinental Airport (IAH).
